extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the uphill finish at Lausanne with a late sprint past MATTHEWS, while POGAČAR gained 4 seconds on GC rivals via third-place time bonus.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT positioned behind MATTHEWS in final 500m then exploited superior sprint to win despite Matthews' lead-out advantage.
MCNULTY paced POGAČAR through steepest 12% gradient section at 1km, revealing UAE's willingness to burn domestiques for micro-gains.
POGAČAR deliberately sprinted for third place time bonus rather than contesting win, prioritizing GC accumulation over stage glory.
O'CONNOR lost 9+ minutes suggesting COVID or mechanical disaster, exposing AG2R's GC campaign vulnerability to single-day collapses.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Won second stage from reduced sprint after 8000ft climbing day, confirming exceptional versatility across terrain profiles.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Second consecutive runner-up finish reveals consistent positioning but inability to convert against top-tier finishers on uphill drags.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Tactically mature decision to sprint for 4-second time bonus rather than expend energy contesting stage win.
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: Provided late high-tempo pacing through 12% gradient at 1km, showing UAE domestique depth on steep finishes.
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: neutral: Present in final but not contesting, suggesting team focus remains on alternative opportunities rather than uphill sprints.
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: Lost 9+ minutes destroying GC ambitions, exposing fragility despite top-10 position entering stage.
rider_signals.GAUDU David: neutral: Mentioned as GC contender present but no tactical impact shown at finish.
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Positioned in finale but did not contest sprint, indicating stage profile didn't suit his explosive climbing style.
